project_constraints.charset_note: Gate FALHOU: a fonte do jogo não tem diacríticos — pangrama pt-BR renderiza como '@' (evidência in-game: artifacts/char1.png, char2.png). Estratégia adotada: TRANSLITERAÇÃO na gravação (acento→ASCII) no reinsert.py; a tradução canônica mantém os acentos. Ver decision_log.md.

glossary_subset.notes: Unidade militar do Ukon -> 'Coorte do Ukon'. Traduzir 'Cohort'->'Coorte' consistente.
Nome dado ao protagonista por Kuon em 11_02 (0x12668). reveal_timing: cena 11_02. Antes disso ele é sem nome.
Rótulo de falante (memória); identidade não revelada — genérico.

Erudito/escriba/mago comico e franzino do grupo do Ukon (cap.12); magia de fogo com cantico+danca longos. Fala em registro ARCAICO e comico (pleaseth/mine own; gag de cicio s->sh). Gruda no Haku como 'benfeitor'.
Soberano de Yamato ('o grande Mikado'); presenteou o moinho a vila. No cap.12 = o imperador/titulo. SPOILER: identidade pessoal e reveal posterior — NAO antecipar. reveal_timing: alem do cap.12.
= Ukon (reveal 13_08; ver spoiler_ledger). General da Direita / Imperial Guard. Twist final em quarentena (beyond_frontier).
Princesa de Kujyuri, filha de Ozen. Timida; gosta de BL; desenha e cozinha bem. Anda com a ave Cocopo. Fonte: corpus cap.13 + wiki.

Guerreiro espirituoso e cativante que faz amizade com Haku ao ve-lo consertar o moinho (cap.12); lidera a 'Coorte do Ukon'. Maroro o trata por 'Mestre Ukon'. SPOILER: identidade verdadeira e reveal de arco posterior — NAO antecipar; no cap.12 e um personagem por si so. reveal_timing: alem do cap.12.

lines: Características: acolhe sem pressionar ("Apenas relaxe..."); curiosa mas paciente; calor humano.
Red flags: soar fria, clínica ou impaciente; formalidade excessiva.
Haku (protagonista, narração 1ª pessoa) — `voice_criticality: high`. Predomina nas 2 cenas:
Características: sentenças curtas e definitivas; duplo sentido ("um mundo totalmente novo te espera"); ambiguidade ameaçadora.
Red flags: soar caloroso/paternal; resolver a ambiguidade (a fala deve permanecer dúbia).
Registro: erudito cômico, pomposo e arcaico; bajulador do Ukon; alívio cômico.
Características: fala arcaica/empolada (equivalente a "vós/peço-vos/meus olhos não me enganam") + gag de cicio (s→sh, ex.: "SHEEKETH"/"shir"); melodramático; gruda no Haku como "benfeitor".
Red flags: perder o arcaísmo ou o cicio (são a piada); soar moderno/neutro; explicar a comédia. Manter o contraste entre a pompa do vocabulário e a fragilidade do personagem.

Oshtor — `voice_criticality: high`. = Ukon até 13_08 (ver spoiler_ledger). Registro formal, nobre, comedido; General da Direita. Antes do reveal, traduzir como o mercenário "Ukon" (espirituoso, informal) — NÃO antecipar a pompa de general
Características: frases quebradas por reticências; perguntas curtas ("Quem... é você...?"); pouca pontuação forte.
Red flags: falas fluentes/articuladas demais; perder o tom de torpor; pontuação "limpa" que apaga a fragmentação.
Rulutieh — `voice_criticality: medium`. Princesa tímida e gentil; hesitante (gagueja quando nervosa: "P-Princesa..."), educada, se anima ao falar de arte/BL. Não soar arrogante apesar de princesa.
Registro: guerreiro carismático, espirituoso, caloroso e informal; líder nato que trata Haku como um irmão mais novo.
Características: desembaraçado, brincalhão, generoso; fala direta e cativante; autoridade leve (lidera a Coorte) sem arrogância.
Red flags: soar rígido/formal/militar demais (a graça é justamente o contraste com a patente); frieza. SPOILER: não deixar a tradução insinuar a identidade verdadeira — no cap.12 ele é só "Ukon".

decisions.title: Opcode de RÓTULO DE FALANTE `53 00` + reconcile de speaker (data-driven)
decisions.summary: **Problema:** in-game, o rótulo de falante aparecia em **inglês** ("Girl") mesmo com a tradução ("Garota") aprovada e gravada. RE: o nome do falante usa um **2º opcode de ponteiro, `53 00`** (mesmo formato file-relativo do `50 00` de diálogo), que o conector ignorava. Resultado: "Girl"→"Garota"

decisions.title: Gate de charset pt-BR — método e veredito
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: likely` e exigir confirmação in-game antes de produção. **Alternativas consideradas:** - Confirmar por presença no texto-fonte — **insuficiente**: o fonte é inglês e quase não usa acentos (só `õ` e `À` aparecem em texto real).

decisions.title: Anomalia 0x33f9 — texto PT/EN corrompido na fonte
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar a linha `0x33f9` como anomalia de fonte (não traduzir em cima do lixo; tratar como linha de sistema reescrita). **Razão:** A string original já vem misturada PT/EN e truncada ("...SISTEMAS AM . RESTARTING...") no próprio jogo. Não é erro de extração. O conector deve sinali

decisions.title: Charset — transliteração na gravação (gate FALHOU)
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Marcar `target_charset_supported: false` e **transliterar** (acento → ASCII) na gravação do binário. A tradução canônica (`approved_translations.csv`, `translation_plan.json`) **mantém os acentos** (correta para QA/revisão); apenas os bytes escritos no jogo são dobrados para ASCI

decisions.title: Escopo cognitivo — 75 → 1025 linhas (cenas 11_01 + 11_02); reveal de Haku in-corpus
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Re-rodar o pipeline completo em escala (cenas 11_01 + 11_02 = 1025 linhas). Novos termos canônicos: **Kuon** (nome revelado em 0x108db), **Haku** (nome dado ao protagonista em 0x12668 — reveal agora **dentro do corpus**), **Tatari** (criatura imortal), **aperyu** (vestimenta), **

decisions.title: CORREÇÃO CRÍTICA — ponteiros são FILE-RELATIVOS, não absolutos
decisions.summary: **Decisão tomada:** Ao investigar o "opcode de início de bloco", descobri que **`50 00`+uint32 é um offset RELATIVO ao início do arquivo (Pack)**, não absoluto. Endereço da string = `file_start_do_site + uint32`. Prova: dos ~47k sites, **42.101** só apontam para string como file-relativos vs **63**

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: Well, guess the sun still rises no matter where\n
tm_voice.target: Enfim, o sol nasce em qualquer lugar, pelo visto\n

tm_voice.speaker: Haku
tm_voice.source: I am. Still... What am I supposed to do now...?
tm_voice.target: Pois é. Mesmo assim... O que é que eu faço agora...?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, you there! Could you spare a moment?
tm_voice.target: Ei, você aí! Pode me dar um momento?

tm_voice.speaker: Garota
tm_voice.source: Hey, I'm sorry for bothering you, but could I ask\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: Master Ukon! It pleaseth my heart to report my\n
tm_voice.target: Mestre Ukon! É com grande satisfação que reporto que meus\n

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: belongings lay duly unpack'd, and await porters.
tm_voice.target: meus pertences estão desfeitos e aguardam os carregadores.

tm_voice.speaker: Maroro
tm_voice.source: I am VERY tired, sir. Naught more now do I desire\n
tm_voice.target: Estou MUITO cansado, senhor. Nada mais desejo agora\n

tm_voice.speaker: Ukon
tm_voice.source: Really, Maroro? Seems like you get tired quicker\n
tm_voice.target: É sério, Maroro? Parece que você se cansa mais rápido\n

tm_voice.speaker: Homem
tm_voice.source: The way you were carrying on, you got us all\n
tm_voice.target: Do jeito que você estava, nos deixou todos\n

tm_voice.speaker: Rulutieh
tm_voice.source: That's a relief... Come on, Cocopo. We'll just be\n
tm_voice.target: Ainda bem... Vamos, Cocopo. Só estamos\n

spoiler_guards.fact: O destino de Oshtor e a transferencia de sua mascara/identidade (fim do jogo). Mantido em QUARENTENA (ratificado pelo usuario).
spoiler_guards.guard: Trate Oshtor como o General da Direita vivo e atuante. NAO antecipe morte, sacrificio, heranca de mascara, nem que outro personagem assumira sua identidade. Sem foreshadowing desse desfecho.

spoiler_guards.fact: A identidade pessoal do Mikado por tras do titulo.
spoiler_guards.guard: Trate o Mikado apenas como o soberano/titulo, a distancia. NAO antecipe vinculo pessoal com nenhum personagem.

lines.source: Even in lands across the sea, people speak of the\n
Twin Shields, Lords Oshtor and Mikazuchi...
That aura he projects... He might even be on par\n
with...
I never imagined he'd be such a great, noble man.
If he's earning that kind of praise from Kuon...
Is he really all that?
Mm. There's that, yes, but I'm just... struck by\n
how much of this world is still unfamiliar to me.
Kuon speaks distantly, her eyes following the man\n
himself as he rides away with his troops.
Ah...
With a long-held exhale, Rulutieh slumps to the\n
ground like a puppet with strings suddenly cut.
Are you OK?
Ah... It feels like all the strength in my body\n
is gone...
I don't blame you, after all that. I know you were\n
scared, but it's gonna be all right now. Promise.
N-No, I meant...
It was just nervewracking... r-really speaking\n
with Sir Oshtor himself.
So that's why.
I understand he's an important guy and all, but\n
you're a princess, Rulutieh.
Is he so high up the chain as that?
N-No, I'm... nothing, next to him.
Lord Oshtor began as but a footman unrank'd, and\n
as a man unthinkably young aspire'd to the mantle\n
of Imperial Guard.
A more humble man--a man so loved amongst his\n
peers--th'art unlikely to meet.
I'faith, milady Rulutieh's quaking at his presence\n
hath warrant, for even the Mikado laudeth his name\n
with honors!
Alas, there are those who brand milord an upstart,\n
but Maroro--Maroro knoweth far better.
So he's like the ideal folk hero.
Verily so. Great pride hath Maroro in his young\n
lordship!
Why would you be proud?
Ah--merely that his lordship and I share in a\n
benefactor. Naught more complicated than that.
I'm not sure I get it, but if you say so.
Another thing, though, has anyone seen Ukon? He's\n
still not back--
Did I hear my name?
The members of Ukon's company who had gone to meet\n
with the strike team reappear in timely fashion.
You sure took your sweet time. We didn't exactly\n
have it easy, back here.
My bad. We ran into some delays securing the cargo\n
again.
Ukon points over his shoulder, and sure enough,\n
the caravan is back together again.
Transporting this stuff safely is our top\n
priority, y'know.
How fared thy strike at the brigands' hold,\n
Master Ukon?
Ah, the surprise attack went almost TOO well.\n
The guidance from the strike team was perfect.
We've got a few wounded, but no casualties.\n
Everybody's fine.
But hey, I heard something about you capturing\n
the bandits' leader?
Oho, so the good tidings have reached thine ears.\n
All praise unto Master Haku for his triumph!
I was just looking out for myself.
C'mon, what're you talking about? I thought I was\n
putting you nice and safely out of the way, but...
You went and stole the show. I'm surprised you\n
rounded them up like that, honestly.
What? No, we just got caught up in a crazy\n
coincidence, that's all.
Hey, don't be so modest! Makes you seem like\n
you're being sarcastic, y'know? Gwahahaha!
It all fell into place by chance, but I don't\n
know if that's good luck or bad luck...
I'm sorry for putting Rulutieh in danger, though.\n
I really didn't mean for that to happen.
Well, all's well that ends well. Besides, it's a\n
nice prestige booster for the princess.
You're really OK with it? It doesn't seem like the\n
kind of thing to subject a princess to...
Circumstances might demand she go out into battle\n
sooner or later. This was some experience for her,\n
if nothin' else.
Prestige, huh...
Now, if only she'd gain a little confidence from\n
this...
Ukon mumbles to himself.
Did you say something?
Nah, it's nothing. Just talking to myself.
All that aside, I'll bet there's a big reward in\n
store for the guy who caught the bandit chief!
Come to think of it, he did mention a "formal\n
commendation" or something like that...
We're talking big. Probably enough for another\n
celebration, even if you split it a few ways.
S-Speakest thou true?
Yeah, I think it's safe to expect at least that\n
much.
H-Huzzah! Fate doth smile upon Maroro this day!
Maroro suddenly bursts with such joy that he\n
nearly starts dancing.
Well, someone's in high spirits, huh?
Though it doth shame me to admit, mine accounts\n
of late are starved of gold to fill them.
But lo, fortune ever turns! I-I may yet avail\n
myself of a new inkstone, brushes, letter-paper!
Maroro puts on a dopey smile at the thought of\n
his newfound wealth, imagining.
Dahahaha! I see. Look forward to it, then.
Ukon laughs, patting Maroro's shoulder, though\n
his expression is... odd.
Man
Man, we gotta get Lord Maroro to treat us to a\n
drink!
You idiot--
Oh, crap, right...
The men surrounding Maroro, muttering, suddenly\n
clam up.
Hm? There wasn't anything particularly offensive\n
about that comment...
Eh? What ails ye? A filled cup is hardly so great\n
an expense, with such riches to my name.
Despite Maroro's generous offer, the men shuffle\n
their feet, looking uncomfortable.
Wh-What is the matter, friends?
N-No worries. We appreciate the offer, though...
Thou needst not hold to such modesty! Let us eat,\n
drink, and be merry together!
Maroro insists, but the way the men decline him\n
seems almost... pitying.
Th-Thou desirest not such revels?
Prithee! Master Haku! Thy bosom company amidst\n
our merry-making would be most welcome!
Well, I won't refuse being treated to a drink.\n
Especially a celebratory one.
Why won't you guys accept his invitation, though?\n
Wouldn't it be gracious to drink with him?
It's not like we don't appreciate the sentiment.\n
It's just that we know he's been saving money...
Yeah. Wouldn't it be better to use that money for\n
your family's debt?
Th-This is so, but...
If Maroro hath aught to offer, let him avail each\n
man of a cup of finest sake, at least.
You'll need money again after that, right? Boss\n
said your family asked him to repair the roof\n
again...
Eh? What's this thou speakest?
...I thought so. You didn't know, huh?
Yeah, and if I recall, they ordered a whole bunch\n
of textiles, too.
Wh-What? Thou speakest truth?
Y-Yeah, I'm afraid so...
N-No! Say not such words to me. Say 'tis a lie...
Maroro looks at Ukon imploringly.
Um... Well. We all have to wake up from dreams\n
eventually...
A-And so Maroro descendeth into the torment of\n
debt once more...
Sorry, man. I couldn't bring myself to tell you.
Maroro sinks to the ground as though sapped of\n
life, Ukon's words a final blow.
Only the sounds of the forest break the silence.
...All right. I've had about enough of this\n
somber mood.
Hey, don't get so down. It'll be my treat,\n
instead.
T-Truly?
Sure. We're friends, aren't we?
I don't really get what's going on, but I can buy\n
the guy a drink. I can't watch him be so pitiful.
That's very generous of you, Haku.
But... I suppose it's fine. You earned this money\n
yourself, after all.
And I think it's very admirable to help a friend\n
like that.
I'll just take it out of your allowance, shall I?
Allowance?
Ah... Right. As long as I'm taking care of you,\n
I'll be managing your finances. You'll get an\n
allowance.
N-No, wait a second, why an allow--
If I leave you in charge of your money, you're\n
going to blow it all on spending. You know it's\n
true.
Hngh... That's not true. Come on.
You even said you'd treat everyone to drinks, so\n
I think it's a fair assumption to make.
This is why I like you, kid. Hear that, boys?\n
Haku's footing the bill for everyone!
At Ukon's suggestion, everyone erupts into cheers.
Wha? Wait a second. I said I'd treat MARORO, not\n
the whole company!
You can't just say "it'll be my treat" in front\n
of everyone and only treat one person.
This is exactly what I'm talking about. I'll make\n
an exception this time and cover you. THIS time.
We'll make it a blowout at our usual place in the\n
capital!
Ukon's Cohorts
Thanks for the treat!
Wh--Who said anything about--
M-Master Haku! Thou wouldst go to such ends for\n
me... Thy charity warmeth the cockles of my heart!
Maroro clings to me as tears stream freely down\n
his face.
How... did it come to this...?
